input.query: Session tokens visible in browser localStorage causing security issues

lessons.title: Auth tokens belong in httpOnly cookies, not localStorage
lessons.content: Tokens placed in localStorage are reachable by any script on the page; httpOnly cookies are not. The session XSS attack surface drops to zero.

lessons.title: XSS attacks can read browser localStorage
lessons.content: Any compromised script — including third-party deps — can read localStorage. Treat it as public.

lessons.title: Session management in Next.js apps
lessons.content: Sessions belong server-side; the browser only carries an opaque identifier.

lessons.title: Tailwind dark mode with class strategy
lessons.content: Use `dark:` variants and toggle a class on html to switch themes.
